heading: Grants for lead poisoning related activities
label: 247b–16 Grants for lead poisoning related activities

children.label: A
children.text: effective, ongoing outreach and community education targeted to families most likely to be at risk for lead poisoning;

children.label: B
children.text: individual family education activities that are designed to reduce ongoing exposures to lead for children with elevated blood lead levels, including through home visits and coordination with other programs designed to identify and treat children at risk for lead poisoning; and

children.label: C
children.text: the development, coordination and implementation of community-based approaches for comprehensive lead poisoning prevention from surveillance to lead hazard control.

children.heading: State match
children.label: 2 State match
children.text: A State is not eligible for a grant under this section unless the State agrees to expend (through State or local funds) $1 for every $2 provided under the grant to carry out the activities described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Application
children.label: 3 Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, a State shall submit an application to the Secretary in such form and manner and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Coordination with other children’s programs
children.label: b Coordination with other children’s programs

children.text: other programs operated in the State that serve children with elevated blood lead levels, including any such programs operated under title V, XIX, or XXI of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 1396 et seq., 1397aa et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 701

children.label: A
children.text: the child welfare and foster care and adoption assistance programs under parts B and E of title IV of such Act [ et seq., 670 et seq.];42 U.S.C. 620

children.label: B
children.text: the head start program established under the Head Start Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: C
children.text: the program of assistance under the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants and children (WIC) under ;section 1786 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: local public and private elementary or secondary schools; or

children.label: E
children.text: public housing agencies, as defined in .section 1437a of this title

children.heading: Performance measures
children.label: c Performance measures
children.text: The Secretary shall establish needs indicators and performance measures to evaluate the activities carried out under grants awarded under this section. Such indicators shall be commensurate with national measures of maternal and child health programs and shall be developed in consultation with the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2001 through 2005.
